meta.name: Indian Goat Curry
meta.subtitle: Slow-simmered (or pressure-cooked) goat in a deep onion-tomato masala

source.name: SSA canonical cuisines/indian/recipes/goat_curry.json
notes: Canonical baseline goat curry (North-Indian style).
Designed for deterministic adaptation: pressure-cooker optional path, tadka/bloom-spices, onion-tomato masala, finish-acid balance.
Goat benefits from longer cook time; pressure cooker provides weeknight feasibility.

summary: A foundational Indian goat curry built on a deeply browned onion-tomato masala with warm spices. Slow-simmer until tender, or use a pressure cooker for faster tenderness. Finish with lemon (and optional yogurt) to balance richness.

yield.notes: Serves 6 as a main with rice/flatbread; 8 with sides.

optional.label: Pressure cooker / Instant Pot
optional.notes: Recommended for faster tenderness (35–45 minutes under pressure), then reduce sauce.

signals.freezerFriendly: yes (freeze after cooling; finish acid after reheating for best brightness)

notes: Dairy is optional if finishing with yogurt or ghee.
Use oil only + skip yogurt (or use dairy-free yogurt) to keep dairy-free.

ingredients.label: Goat stew meat (bone-in preferred)
notes: Bone-in adds body to the gravy.
If using boneless, reduce cook time slightly and monitor tenderness.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Season in layers; final adjustment at the end.

ingredients.label: Ghee
ingredients.notes: Optional; classic richness. Use all oil to keep dairy-free.

ingredients.label: Cumin seeds
ingredients.notes: Optional but recommended for a more aromatic base.

ingredients.label: Yellow onions, thinly sliced
ingredients.notes: About 4 cups sliced; browning these is key.

ingredients.label: Green chili, slit (optional)
ingredients.notes: Serrano or Thai chili; omit for mild.

ingredients.label: Kashmiri chili powder (or mild chili powder)
ingredients.notes: Adjust heat; Kashmiri is more color than burn.

ingredients.label: Water or stock
notes: Use enough to barely cover for stovetop braise; reduce/add as needed.
Pressure-cooker path uses slightly less to prevent thin sauce.

ingredients.label: Garam masala
ingredients.notes: Add near the end for best aroma.

ingredients.label: Plain yogurt (optional)
notes: Optional for a slightly creamy tang.
Temper and add off-heat to prevent curdling.

ingredients.label: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Add at end; can substitute lime, amchur, or a small tamarind splash.

steps.title: Prep and salt the goat
instructions: Trim any large, hard pieces of fat/sinew from the goat (do not remove all connective tissue).
Season goat with 1.5 tsp of the salt and let sit 10 minutes while you prep aromatics.
Slice onions; mince garlic; grate ginger; slit chili if using.

steps.title: Bloom whole spices (optional but recommended)
instructions: Heat a heavy pot over medium heat.
Add oil (and ghee if using).
If using whole spices: add cumin seeds, cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon; stir 20–30 seconds until fragrant.
checks: Spices should be fragrant, not scorched; lower heat if needed.

steps.title: Deeply brown the onions
instructions: Add sliced onions and a pinch of salt.
Cook 15–20 minutes, stirring often, until onions are deeply golden to light brown.
If onions start sticking, add 1–2 tbsp water and scrape up browned bits (fond).
checks: Onions should be deeply golden; this builds the curry’s depth and body.
Avoid burning—use medium/medium-low and deglaze as needed.

steps.title: Add aromatics and bloom ground spices
instructions: Add green chili (if using), garlic, and ginger; cook 60 seconds until fragrant.
Add ground cumin, coriander, turmeric, Kashmiri chili, and black pepper.
Stir constantly 20–30 seconds to bloom spices without burning.
checks: Spices should smell toasted and aromatic, not bitter.

steps.title: Cook down the tomato masala
instructions: Add crushed tomatoes and stir well.
Simmer uncovered 10–15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and darker, with oil separating at the edges.
This removes raw tomato flavor and concentrates the masala.
checks: Masala should be thick enough that it does not look watery.
Oil separation at the edges is a good doneness cue.

steps.title: Coat and sear the goat in the masala
instructions: Add goat and stir to coat thoroughly.
Cook 6–8 minutes, stirring, to lightly sear the goat and marry it with the masala.

steps.title: Braise until tender (stovetop path)
instructions: Add water/stock and bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer 75–105 minutes, stirring every 15–20 minutes.
Add small splashes of water if the sauce gets too thick before the goat is tender.
checks: Goat is done when it is fork-tender and connective tissue has softened.
Keep the simmer gentle; rapid boiling can toughen meat.

steps.title: Pressure cooker option (Instant Pot path)
instructions: If using a pressure cooker: after searing goat in the masala, add 2.0 cups water/stock (slightly less than stovetop).
Lock lid and cook on High Pressure 35–45 minutes (older goat may need 50 minutes).
Natural release 10–15 minutes, then quick release remaining pressure.
Switch to sauté mode and simmer uncovered to reduce sauce to desired thickness.
checks: Sauce may be thinner after pressure cooking—reduce uncovered.
If goat isn’t tender, pressure cook 8–10 minutes more.

steps.title: Finish, balance, and serve
instructions: When goat is tender, uncover and simmer 5–10 minutes to thicken sauce if needed.
Stir in garam masala and simmer 1 minute.
Turn off heat. Add lemon juice.
Optional yogurt finish (anti-curdle): temper yogurt with a few spoonfuls of hot curry liquid, then stir in off-heat.
Taste and adjust salt, heat, and acidity. Garnish with cilantro if using.
checks: Add yogurt off-heat to prevent curdling.
If curry tastes heavy, add a touch more lemon or a pinch of salt.

notes: Cool quickly; store covered.
Goat curry tastes even better the next day.
Freeze after cooling; thaw overnight in fridge.
Reheat gently; add lemon after reheating for best brightness.

leftoversPlan: Day 2: serve over rice with cucumber-onion salad.
Day 3: reduce sauce and serve as a hearty curry wrap with flatbread.
batchNotes: Excellent for batch cooking; flavor improves overnight.
Freeze portions for future quick meals; finish acid after reheating.

notes: Goat is safe when cooked through; tenderness requires time/pressure for collagen breakdown.
Cool leftovers within 2 hours; reheat until steaming hot.
If using yogurt, add off-heat and avoid boiling after adding.
Emphasize whole spices (cardamom, clove, cinnamon).
Keep chili mild; add extra garam masala at the end.
Replace yogurt with 3/4 cup coconut milk at the end.
Optional: add curry leaves if available.
Use the pressure-cooker option for tenderness.
Use crushed tomatoes (not fresh) and reduce sauce on sauté after pressure.
